findings.evidence: Line 120 declares `abbrev fiberMulAction ... : MulAction ... where`; lines 133-155 already provide pointwise API, so consumers need not unfold the action body.
findings.fix: Make this an opaque `def`, or inline it into `instFiberMulAction` and keep only the instance plus the `fiber_smul_*` API.
findings.issue: `fiberMulAction` is a public `abbrev`, so downstream code can rely on the reducible implementation of the instance instead of the stated action lemmas.

findings.evidence: Line 148 states `lemma fiber_smul_mem (φ : Deck p) (e : p ⁻¹' {b}) : p (φ • e : E) = b`; this is the normal form for projection after the fibre action.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `fiber_smul_mem` or provide an equivalent `[simp]` lemma for `p (φ • e : E) = b`.
findings.issue: The characteristic lemma that the restricted action stays over `b` is not marked `[simp]`, so the public action cannot normalize fibre projection goals automatically.

summary: The fibre action API is mostly characterized, but it exposes an implementation body unnecessarily and leaves the main fibre-preservation lemma out of simp automation.
